source_type: Game Accessibility Guidelines; collaborative specialist reference
verified_claim: All UI areas must be reachable with the same input method as gameplay. Complementary evidence: Android recommends 48dp touch targets for interactive elements, AoE IV supports HUD text scaling and contrast, and WCAG requires controls for non-essential persistent motion.
applicability: Input parity, reachable focus/activation/back behavior, touch target size, contrast, text scale, and reduced-motion support are universal web-game constraints.
battle_screen_implication: Keep semantic buttons and visible labels for every actionable heraldic icon. Ensure keyboard focus, Enter/Space, Escape/back, touch targets of at least 48dp, high-contrast text, and a static reduced-motion reading path for all tactical state.
anti_pattern: Decorative icon-only actions, right-click-only group orders, low-contrast parchment text, or indefinite glows/shakes carrying unique game state.
acceptance_criterion: Keyboard-only and touch-only test passes can perform every current battle command and dismiss transient UI; all persistent tactical state remains readable when reduced motion is enabled.
